audit_philosophy: Negative filter — flags removals to reject. 'Not flagged' does NOT mean approved; engineering verification required.
calibration_caveats: Junction detector threshold (20 high-density elements) and tunnel detector thresholds (2.5:1 aspect ratio, 3.0x asymmetry) are absolute, calibrated to ~3 mm mesh spacing. Coarser meshes may cause junction detector to under-trigger; verify rib/wall intersections manually. Statistical-floor calibration is articulated as future work.

regions.pattern_rationale: Mixed neighborhood (90% high-density), aspect 1.6:1; standard removal pattern

mfg_filter.rationale: Geometry compatible with single-action injection moulding (continuous walls, no detected undercuts or tunnels).

struct_filter.rationale: Structural junction detected: removal region sits at a 3D corner (three or more structural members meet). Surrounding high-density material extends in multiple directions (principal singular values: 1.00 / 0.75 / 0.39). Material at structural junctions routes loads between the joined members; removing it weakens the entire load path even if the region's current FoS appears acceptable.

func_filter.rationale: No manual functional zones defined (complexity filter provides primary functional protection).

cplx_filter.rationale: Region complexity within baseline (mean 0.953, 5% above baseline, 194 high-complexity elements within 18 mm).

regions.pattern_rationale: Mixed neighborhood (96% high-density), aspect 2.9:1; standard removal pattern

mfg_filter.rationale: Tunnel detected: elongated region (aspect ratio 2.9:1) with strongly asymmetric surroundings. One end at (391, 882, 1279) is buried in high-density material (287 dense neighbors within 5.0 mm), the other end at (409, 829, 1304) opens to a cavity (80 dense neighbors). The solver is attempting to tunnel through structural material to reach an interior space — manufacturing this would require undercut tooling and structurally compromises the wall the tunnel passes through.
